name: Arpeggiator
description: This algorithm is a very flexible arpeggiator, in some respects an interactive, performance-oriented sequencer. It is based on the arpeggiator in our FH-2 module. It takes MIDI or CV/gate input and generates MIDI and/or CV/gate output. The key to using this as an interactive sequencer is to map the ‘Commands’ parameters, so you can use them alongside playing notes to manipulate your patterns.
gui_description: The display shows various information relating to the current arpeggiation pattern. On the left you see the current output note, whether the pattern is currently latched, and the current root note. The three lines in the main section of the display show, from top to bottom, a representation of the entire arpeggiation pattern, the octaves of the pattern (when ‘Range’ is used), and the note within a single octave of the pattern. For the ‘as played’ modes, the lowest line instead shows actual note numbers. A ‘--’ represents a rest added with the ‘Rest’ command. When ‘Truncate’ is used, the omitted steps are greyed out.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The arpeggiation mode

parameters.name: Range
parameters.description: The number of octaves to repeat the arpeggiation pattern over.

parameters.name: Latch
parameters.description: Enables latching. Notes are held until all keys are released and a new note played.

parameters.name: Truncate
parameters.description: Shortens the arpeggiation pattern by only playing the first portion, according to the percentage set.

parameters.name: Gate type
parameters.description: Sets the gate type: “Trigger” or “% of clock”.

parameters.name: Length (ms)
parameters.description: Sets the length of the gate output if the type is “Trigger”.

parameters.name: Length (%)
parameters.description: Sets the length of the gate output if the type is “% of clock”.

parameters.name: Add
parameters.description: While enabled, incoming notes will be added to the arpeggiation pattern. This is most useful when the Mode is ‘As Played’ and Latch is on.

parameters.name: Remove
parameters.description: While enabled, incoming notes will be removed from the pattern (if the note in question is actually in the pattern already).

parameters.name: Rest
parameters.description: When enabled, a rest is added to or removed from the arpeggiation pattern, according to whether Add or Remove is currently active.

parameters.name: Transpose
parameters.description: While enabled, incoming notes will transpose the current pattern, by the amount of the difference between the incoming note and the root note of the pattern.

parameters.name: Transpose in key
parameters.description: Like Transpose, but rather than a straight chromatic transposition, the pattern is transposed within the key defined by the root note.

parameters.name: Root note
parameters.description: While enabled, an incoming note overrides the root note, so that subsequent transposition is based on the overridden note.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel to listen on.

parameters.name: MPE channels
parameters.description: Controls how the algorithm will respond to MPE.

parameters.name: I2C channel
parameters.description: Sets the I2C channel.

parameters.name: Sustain
parameters.description: Directly controls the sustain (like a MIDI sustain pedal).

parameters.name: Sustain mode
parameters.description: The standard polysynth sustain mode parameter.

parameters.name: Reset mode
parameters.description: The reset mode, one of ‘Trigger’ or ‘Run/stop’.

parameters.name: Follow MIDI clock
parameters.description: Sets whether the arpeggiator follows MIDI clock.

parameters.name: Divisor
parameters.description: Sets the clock divisor when following MIDI clock.

parameters.name: Auto reset
parameters.description: Sets a number of quarter notes after which the arpeggiator will automatically reset to step 1 (only available when following MIDI clock).

parameters.name: MIDI channel (out)
parameters.description: The output MIDI channel.

parameters.name: Output to breakout
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the breakout.

parameters.name: Output to Select Bus
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the Select Bus.

parameters.name: Output to USB
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to USB.

parameters.name: Output to internal
parameters.description: Enables internal MIDI output – that is, MIDI is sent to the other algorithms.

parameters.name: Gate mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector for the gate output.

parameters.name: Pitch mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector for the pitch output.

parameters.name: Velocity mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector for the velocity output.

input_ports.name: MIDI input
input_ports.description: MIDI input for notes and control

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the clock input.

input_ports.name: Reset input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the reset input.

output_ports.name: MIDI output
output_ports.description: MIDI output to breakout, USB, or internal algorithms

output_ports.name: Gate output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the gate output.

output_ports.name: Pitch output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the pitch output.

output_ports.name: Velocity output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the velocity output.
